Hello,
I don't know if this is a bug, a mistranslation or just me that don't understood correctly.
However Pickman Chromaphage card say "if inspiration is triggered gain 2 additional instances of damage" 
So for me if I use inspiration twice during the turn (Rouse + Exalt), the instances of damage should go up to 7 but it's locked to 5.
Plus, the translation between the in fight card and the awaken tab card is different which make it really misleading. (see screenshot)
On one side you understand that the + damage instance will carry over turn but on the other side you're not even sure. Well it doesn't carry anyway and I don't mind it, but I suppose this part is more about mistranslation and I should mention it.

Also, the name "Fantasia" whenever you use his Rouse or trigger exalt with 10 creativity is named "Frenzy" on character buff list.

content:
When thinking about what to upgrade, it helps to see what bonuses the next level provides, be that for units‘s cards or for Wheels‘s effects. Currently, you can only tap on „Next“ once to see what the next level gives you. I suggest adding the ability to be able to see what you get for all level ups (could be by being able to click the „Next“ button multiple times) to help with choices on what you want to invest in.
For example, do I want to increase the level of a certain card for a unit or will that card give me overall worse stats for my investment than another card would? Should I even level that one to begin with or does it make more sense to, idk level the exalt or something. Do I want to go for copies of a certain SSR wheel or are the effects not good enough? 
Such questions arise when you try to plan around your limited resources and such a feature would help a lot with that.
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Add ability to see all card levels

Hello,
With the help of the community I tried to find out the formula that outputs the final stats of the relics and orisons in the game, since one of the Pickman's skill depends on it, but, alas, it's harder than I would have thought.

At best, I can say that it scales much faster toward the last 10 levels, it caps at 70, perhaps it doesn't increase at every levels but at some intervals, and at least the number of posses increases the final stats on last step of the formula.

But I'll give in and ask directly, is it possible to have details about how it scales based on levels and number of posse? The only ingame maths we have is that each posse increases the final stats by 1%, up to a max of 50%.
I have already provided feedback on the latest situation and will contact you promptly if there is a solution🙏
